s2.ptxt: Inspect the cylinder bore and the piston for rust or scoring. If necessary, replace the rear disc brake cylinder assembly and piston.
Using a ruler, measure the pad lining thickness.
If the pad lining thickness is less than the minimum, replace the brake pads.

atten4.ptxt: Be sure to check wear of the front disc after replacing the brake pad with a new one.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT NO. 1 REAR DISC BRAKE PAD SUPPORT PLATE

s2.ptxt: Inspect the No. 1 rear disc brake pad support plate.

atten4.ptxt: Make sure that they have sufficient rebound, and have no deformation, cracks or wear, and all rust and dirt are cleaned off.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT NO. 2 REAR DISC BRAKE PAD SUPPORT PLATE

atten4.ptxt: Inspect the No. 2 rear disc brake pad support plate using the same procedure with the No. 1 rear disc brake pad support plate.

s2.ptxt: Using a micrometer, measure the disc thickness.
If the disc thickness is less than the minimum, replace the rear disc.
Temporarily fasten the rear disc with the 3 hub nuts.
Using a dial indicator, measure the disc runout 10 mm (0.39 in.) away from the outer edge of the rear disc.

If the runout exceeds the maximum value, change the installation positions of the disc and axle so that the runout will become minimal. If the runout exceeds the maximum even after the installation positions are changed, check the bearing play in the axial direction and the axle hub runout (See page ). If the bearing play and the axle hub runout are normal and if the disc thickness is not within the specified range, grind the disc. If the disc thickness is less than the minimum, replace the disc.
